overview: A minimal cloud-native application consisting of a single backend service that exposes a REST API endpoint. The service responds with 'Hello, World!' to incoming HTTP requests, providing a simple health check and verification mechanism for developers.
requirements: The API must respond with 'Hello, World!' when called
The API must be accessible via HTTP/HTTPS
The API must provide a /health endpoint for health checks
The API must return a 200 OK status code on successful requests
The response must be in a standard format (JSON)

components.name: hello-api
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Hello API
  description: A simple API that returns 'Hello, World!'
  version: 1.0.0
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
    description: Local development server
paths:
  /hello:
    get:
      summary: Get hello message
      description: Returns a greeting message
      operationId: getHello
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Successful response
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  message:
                    type: string
                    example: Hello, World!
                required:
                  - message
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      description: Returns the health status of the service
      operationId: healthCheck
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
                required:
                  - status
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a Go-based HTTP API service using the standard net/http library. The service must listen on port 9090. Create two endpoints: (1) GET /hello - returns JSON {"message": "Hello, World!"} with status 200, and (2) GET /health - returns JSON {"status": "ok"} with status 200. Use a standard HTTP router or mux. Set proper Content-Type headers to application/json for all responses. Include graceful shutdown handling for SIGTERM and SIGINT signals. Log incoming requests with method, path, and response status. The Dockerfile should use a multi-stage build with golang:1.21-alpine as builder and alpine:latest as runtime. Copy the binary to /app/hello-api and expose port 9090. The CMD should run the binary. Include error handling for all operations. Ensure the service starts within 5 seconds and responds to requests within 100ms under normal load.
